markets.title: Bitcoin Up or Down - July 13, 10:45PM-10:50PM ET
markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - July 13, 10:45PM-10:50PM ET

raw_category.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the BTC/USDT price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than the price at the beginning of that range.

It will resolve to "Down" if the BTC/USDT price at the end of the time range is lower than the price at the beginning of that range.

If the two prices are exactly equal, the market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USDT data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usdt-topofbook-datalink?timeframe=1d&chart=candlestick.

To verify the final price, use the open price of the candlestick corresponding to the market's end time. For example, for a market running from 1:10 PM ET to 1:15 PM ET, use the open price of the 1:15 PM candlestick as the final price.

The Chainlink stream uses the mid-price, which is calculated as the average price between the bid and ask prices from Binance's Top of Book, converted into the quote currency.

This market is based solely on the price reported by the Chainlink BTC/USDT data stream, not on other sources or spot markets.

In the event that Chainlink data is unavailable, corrupted, or cannot be used to resolve the market, an administrator may resolve the market using Binance data (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). In such a case, the resolution will be based on the close price of the BTC/USDT 1-second candle from Binance corresponding to the relevant timestamps.
2026-07-14T02:45:00Z

This market will resolve to "Up" if the BTC/USDT price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than the price at the beginning of that range.

It will resolve to "Down" if the BTC/USDT price at the end of the time range is lower than the price at the beginning of that range.

If the two prices are exactly equal, the market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USDT data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usdt-topofbook-datalink?timeframe=1d&chart=candlestick.

To verify the final price, use the open price of the candlestick corresponding to the market's end time. For example, for a market running from 1:10 PM ET to 1:15 PM ET, use the open price of the 1:15 PM candlestick as the final price.

The Chainlink stream uses the mid-price, which is calculated as the average price between the bid and ask prices from Binance's Top of Book, converted into the quote currency.

This market is based solely on the price reported by the Chainlink BTC/USDT data stream, not on other sources or spot markets.

In the event that Chainlink data is unavailable, corrupted, or cannot be used to resolve the market, an administrator may resolve the market using Binance data (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). In such a case, the resolution will be based on the close price of the BTC/USDT 1-second candle from Binance corresponding to the relevant timestamps.
2026-07-14T03:30:00Z

It will resolve to "Down" if the BTC/USDT price at the end of the time range is lower than the price at the beginning of that range.

If the two prices are exactly equal, the market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USDT data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usdt-topofbook-datalink?timeframe=1d&chart=candlestick.

To verify the final price, use the open price of the candlestick corresponding to the market's end time. For example, for a market running from 1:10 PM ET to 1:15 PM ET, use the open price of the 1:15 PM candlestick as the final price.

The Chainlink stream uses the mid-price, which is calculated as the average price between the bid and ask prices from Binance's Top of Book, converted into the quote currency.

This market is based solely on the price reported by the Chainlink BTC/USDT data stream, not on other sources or spot markets.

In the event that Chainlink data is unavailable, corrupted, or cannot be used to resolve the market, an administrator may resolve the market using Binance data (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). In such a case, the resolution will be based on the close price of the BTC/USDT 1-second candle from Binance corresponding to the relevant timestamps.
2026-07-14T04:15:00Z
